summary: Renamed many anonymous lbl_/func_ assembly symbols in dolphin DVD, dolphin JPEG, and sysdolphin baselib assembly to descriptive local/global names. The PR mainly improved symbol knowledge for future C decompilation, with one matching source declaration update in psdisptev.c from lbl_804D7950 to prevTev.
what_changed: Labeled DVD error table data: lbl_80400F98 became ErrorTable in asm/dolphin/dvd/dvderror.s, and all @ha/@l references were updated.

Labeled many DVD low-level state globals in asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdlow.s, including AlarmForTimeout, FirstRead, LastLength, ResetCoverCallback, WaitingCoverClose, WorkAroundType, WorkAroundSeekLocation, LastReadFinished, LastReadIssued, and LastCommandWasRead.

Collapsed paired DVD timestamp symbols into 8-byte objects where usage showed +4 high/low-style accesses: LastResetEnd, LastReadFinished, and LastReadIssued are represented with .skip 0x8 and referenced as symbol and symbol+4.
Labeled JPEG decoder symbols in asm/dolphin/jpeg/jpegdec.s, including __THPJpegNaturalOrder, __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY, __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompU, __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompV, Gbase, Gwid, and Gq.

Labeled sysdolphin baselib globals: prevTev in psdisptev.s, arg_buf and arg_buf_size in robj.s, many state_* variables and invalidate_funcs/dark_matter in state.s, and driverInactivatedCallback, driverMasterClockCallback, and driverPauseCallback in synth.s.
Updated src/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisptev.c to use extern s32 prevTev[2] instead of extern s32 lbl_804D7950[2], keeping the C declaration aligned with the newly named assembly symbol.
smart_moves: Renamed both definitions and every assembly reference, including @ha/@l and @sda21 relocations, which keeps symbol names consistent across code and data sections.
Recognized adjacent 32-bit globals that function as larger logical values and renamed them with +4 references rather than leaving separate lbl_ symbols.

Used descriptive state/callback names that encode observed use sites, such as ResetCoverCallback being loaded into r12 and called via mtlr/blrl, and WaitingCoverClose being tested and cleared around cover-close handling.
Propagated the psdisptev assembly rename into the C source extern declaration, avoiding a split-brain state where assembly and C refer to the same object with different names.
decomp_lessons: Even label-only PRs are useful decomp groundwork: replacing lbl_ addresses with semantic names makes later C reconstruction and review much easier.
When an sdata/sbss symbol is consistently accessed as symbol and symbol+4, consider whether it is a paired 64-bit value or compound object before naming it as two unrelated globals.
For assembly-local cleanup, update all relocation forms at once: @ha, @l, @sda21, function branch targets, and data-section .global labels.
If a renamed assembly symbol is declared from C, update the C extern in the same PR to preserve cross-language consistency.
Callback names can often be justified by call patterns: load from global, null-check, move to LR, and blrl is strong evidence for a function pointer callback.
naming_conventions: DVD low-level globals use PascalCase names matching existing nearby style: LastCommandWasRead, LastReadFinished, LastReadIssued, LastResetEnd, AlarmForTimeout.
Workaround DVD state used WorkAround* spelling: WorkAroundType and WorkAroundSeekLocation.
THP/JPEG internals used SDK-like double-underscore names: __THPJpegNaturalOrder and __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY/U/V.
sysdolphin state globals use lowercase snake_case with state_ prefixes: state_blend_type, state_src_factor, state_dst_factor, state_logic_op, state_z_func, state_alpha_comp0, state_alpha_comp1.
Callback globals use camelCase/Pascal-like descriptive suffixes: ResetCoverCallback, driverInactivatedCallback, driverMasterClockCallback, driverPauseCallback.
Temporary/global JPEG variables used short G-prefixed names seen in the diff: Gbase, Gwid, Gq.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed labels only; no evidence in the provided diff of functional code-generation changes beyond symbol naming and data label grouping.
Used symbol+4 references for the second word of multiword globals instead of creating separate adjacent labels, e.g. LastReadFinished+4, LastReadIssued+4, LastResetEnd+4.
Kept section placement intact while renaming .data, .sdata, and .sbss objects.
Renamed function labels in assembly call sites, e.g. calls to func_80334880 became calls to __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY where appropriate.
Adjusted one C extern declaration to match a renamed assembly global, which is a low-risk tactic for preserving linkage while improving readability.

review_feedback.interpretation: Approved despite noting some names were unusual; no requested changes were present in the slice.
Brief non-actionable follow-up comment; no technical guidance in the available text.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that decompile dolphin/dvd/dvdlow.c using LastReadFinished, LastReadIssued, or WorkAroundType.
Search for subsequent JPEG decoder work that relies on __THPJpegNaturalOrder or __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY/U/V names.
Check later sysdolphin baselib state decompilation PRs for reuse of the state_* and invalidate_funcs naming established here.
Verify whether the unusual names mentioned in review were later changed or retained.
